content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
William J. Keese, Esq. 
California Dental Association 
Government Relations Office 
818 K Street Mall 
P. O. Box 13749 
Sacramento, CA 
95853 
Dear Mr. Keese: 
May 13, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-86-l24 
This is in response to your letter of April 14, 1986, and 
to confirm my telephone advice to Brenda Proschaska regarding 
your registration and reporting obligations under the amended 
lobbying disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act 
(Government Code Sections 81000-91015). 
As you know, the amended lobbying disclosure provisions 
contain a new definition of "lobbying firm" which includes 
individual contract lobbyists (Government Code Section 
82038.5). 
As stated in your letter, you are an individual 
contract lobbyist and have registered and will file reports as 
William J. Keese, Inc., a lobbying firm. 
You have also stated that you are a full-time employee and 
lobbyist for the California Dental Association, which is not a 
client of William J. Keese, Inc., and have asked if you may also 
be registered and file reports as an employee-lobbyist for the 
Association. 
We have concluded that you should file reports as a 
lobbying firm only, and report the payments you receive for 
lobbying from the California Dental Association on your Report 
of Lobbying Firm (Form 625). 
If desired, you may register and 
file reports under the name William J. Keese/William J. Keese, 
Inc., and may include a statement on your filings to explain 
that the Association is not a client of William J. Keese, Inc. 
I hope this information is helpful. 
Please let me know if 
I can be of further assistance. 
Sincerely, 
fJ. a~ 
~UaULkU 
Carla J. Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 
CJW:cah 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

California

April 18, 1986 
William J. Keese 
California Dental Association 
P.o. Box 13749 
Sacramento, CA 
95853 
Re: 
86-124 
DE. ... r Mr. Keese: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 .. Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916) 322~566()

sections.question: As an individual contract lobbyist registered as William J. Keese, Inc., may I also register and file reports as an employee-lobbyist for the California Dental Association, which is not a client of William J. Keese, Inc.?
sections.conclusion: You should file reports as a lobbying firm only, and report the payments you receive for lobbying from the California Dental Association on your Report of Lobbying Firm (Form 625). If desired, you may register and file reports under the name William J. Keese/William J. Keese, Inc., and may include a statement on your filings to explain that the Association is not a client of William J. Keese, Inc.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an individual contract lobbyist may simultaneously register and file reports as both a lobbying firm and as an employee-lobbyist for a non-client organization under the amended lobbying disclosure provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the requestor must file reports as a lobbying firm only, reporting California Dental Association payments on Form 625, though the requestor may use a combined name format and include an explanatory statement clarifying the Association's status.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the initial acknowledgment letter (April 18, 1986) and the substantive advice letter (May 13, 1986). The question and conclusion were synthesized from the detailed discussion in the May 13 letter. Some text quality issues in the source document did not impact core content extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As an individual contract lobbyist registered as William J. Keese, Inc., may I also register and file reports as an employee-lobbyist for the California Dental Association, which is not a client of William J. Keese, Inc.?

CONCLUSION: You should file reports as a lobbying firm only, and report the payments you receive for lobbying from the California Dental Association on your Report of Lobbying Firm (Form 625). If desired, you may register and file reports under the name William J. Keese/William J. Keese, Inc., and may include a statement on your filings to explain that the Association is not a client of William J. Keese, Inc.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to William J. Keese's request regarding lobbying registration and reporting obligations under the amended Political Reform Act. The Commission advised that Keese should file reports as a lobbying firm only, reporting his compensation from the California Dental Association on the lobbying firm report form.
